scenarios.1: Auth gate — chat endpoints reject calls without the session cookie
scenarios.2: Validation — empty message / missing repo → 4xx, not 500
scenarios.3: 409 single-flight — second builder POST while one is live → 409
scenarios.4: Ask concurrency — lane=ask runs while a builder run is live (own seq/session)
scenarios.5: Stop — /api/chat/stop returns 200, /api/runs flips terminal, process dies
scenarios.6: Reattach — GET /api/chat/stream?after=N replays with no dupes/gaps, seq increasing
scenarios.7: Runs snapshot — /api/runs reflects running → done/error transitions
scenarios.8: Sessions list/transcript — turn appears; messages read back; traversal id rejected
scenarios.9: Bad inputs — unknown lane/model, malformed/ghost sessionId → graceful error, no hang/500
scenarios.10: Basic turn — short prompt → ordered session → token… → usage? → done
scenarios.11: Resume — second turn with returned sessionId appends to same JSONL
scenarios.12: Tool lifecycle — a forced Read → tool start/input/end with summary/preview/ok
scenarios.13: Model param — claude-haiku-4-5 honoured by the CLI and reflected in the run record
scenarios.14: Ask read-only — an ask-lane write request does not mutate disk (permission-mode plan)

suites.title: Behavioural / protocol
suites.blurb: Auth gate, input validation, stop-when-idle, runs/sessions shape, path traversal. Pure HTTP/SSE protocol — no CLI, no tokens.

suites.title: Smoke (one real turn)
suites.blurb: One cheap real turn to confirm the isolated instance can actually drive the Claude CLI before spending on the full real-run suite.

suites.title: Real run (end-to-end CLI)
suites.blurb: Drives the real Claude CLI: basic/resume/tool/model/ask-read-only plus 409, ask-concurrency, stop and reattach against live runs. Cheap model, tiny prompts.

suites.title: Bad input (bug hunt)
suites.blurb: Malformed real inputs that reach the CLI — ghost session, bad model, unknown lane, malformed sessionId — must reach a clean terminal error, never a hang/500/wedged slot.

findings.title: A user-initiated stop is recorded as status:"error"
findings.detail: RunSession.Complete() marks done only if a done event was seen, else error; a cancel sees none. So a deliberately-stopped run is indistinguishable from a crash, and stop also emits {type:"error",message:"Run stopped by user."}. Repro: start a turn, POST /api/chat/stop, read /api/runs → error. Consider a distinct stopped/cancelled status.

findings.title: Resuming a non-existent session yields an opaque error_during_execution
findings.detail: No session event, no "session not found" hint. Repro: POST /api/chat with a well-formed but unknown sessionId → terminal error with that generic message. Graceful (no hang/crash) but unfriendly.
